summary: Use article discovery, one GDELT bulk supplement, and one fast-moving discussion source for this round while skipping policy and long-tail video sources.

family_plans.reason: Use GDELT article recon plus one anchored bulk supplement for broader public-claim coverage.

layer_plans.reason: Article discovery is the primary entry layer for mission-window public-claim recon.
Add one approved bulk table after recon to enrich themes and locations without over-expanding cost.

family_plans.reason: Video and comment collection are unnecessary for this narrow smoke round.

layer_plans.reason: Video discovery is not necessary for the current public-claim coverage target.
Comment collection is unnecessary without a video-driven evidence gap.

family_plans.reason: Official rulemaking records are not necessary for this public-discussion-heavy smoke round.

layer_plans.reason: Federal Register discovery is out of scope for this round.
Regulations.gov comments remain out of scope because no rulemaking document anchor is needed.

family_plans.reason: Fast-moving public discussion is relevant for diffusion and recency.

layer_plans.reason: One fast-moving discussion source broadens channel diversity without escalating cost.

source_decisions.reason: Use broad article discovery for public-claim coverage.
Event-level export files are unnecessary for this narrow smoke round because article-level discovery should anchor the first pass.
Mention-volume supplement is unnecessary until claim coverage looks too sparse after article discovery.
Use one GKG bulk supplement to add deterministic theme and location coverage from the same mission window.
Fast-moving public discussion is relevant for diffusion and recency.
Video discovery is not necessary for this narrow smoke round.
